subject: Databases
title: B-Tree Index Basics
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: B-Tree Index Basics.

hook.text: A B-tree is a balanced tree structure. Finding any value takes the same number of steps, no matter where it is.

buildup.text: Each node holds sorted keys and pointers. The root splits into branches. Leaves point to actual data rows.

discovery.text: Looking up a value: start at root, compare, go left or right, repeat. Three or four levels reach billions of rows.

twist.text: B-trees are wide and short. A 4-level B-tree with 500 keys per node covers 62 billion entries.

climax.text: B-tree is the default index in Postgres, MySQL, and most relational databases. It handles 90% of use cases.

punchline.text: B-trees: wide, balanced, fast. The workhorse of database indexing.

quiz.question: Why can a B-tree index find any value in just a few steps?
options: It's a wide, balanced tree where each level narrows the search by hundreds of keys
It stores all values in memory
B-trees only work on tables with fewer than 1000 rows
